title: Min Stack
problem_statement: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in constant time.

examples.input: push(-2), push(0), push(-3), getMin(), pop(), top(), getMin()

constraints: At most 3 * 10^4 calls will be made.
intuition: Track the current minimum at each stack level using an auxiliary stack or paired values.

approaches.name: Stack of pairs or dual stacks
approaches.description: Maintain the current minimum alongside each value so getMin can return it instantly.
approaches.why_not: Searching the stack for the minimum each time is O(n) and too slow.

interview_qa.q: How do you retrieve the minimum in O(1)?
interview_qa.a: Store the minimum with each pushed element.
The minimum automatically reverts to the next stored minimum.
